diagrams.title: Process State Transition Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the five states of a process and transitions between them
elements: Arrows showing transitions: New→Ready, Ready→Running, Running→Ready, Running→Waiting, Waiting→Ready, Running→Terminated
diagrams.purpose: Understanding process lifecycle and how processes move through different states in an operating system

diagrams.title: Process Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Shows the memory structure of a process
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how process memory is organized and the difference between process and thread memory sharing

diagrams.title: Scheduling Hierarchy
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how different schedulers work together to manage process execution and system load

diagrams.title: Thread Models Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of Many-to-One, One-to-One, and Many-to-Many threading models
elements: Model 1: Many user threads → 1 kernel thread
Model 3: N user threads → M kernel threads
Arrows showing mapping between user and kernel threads
diagrams.purpose: Understanding different approaches to implementing threads and their trade-offs
